principal_risks.description: If legacy dominance remains too high, micro-probe signal remains ambiguous and scalar rebound interpretation stays confounded.
Aggressive pruning can sever continuity with earlier stages and weaken robustness stress behavior.
Undeclared balancing heuristics can mimic weighting and invalidate interpretability.
Excessive removal of legacy uncertainty cases may reduce behavioral breadth and inflate apparent gains.
Unbalanced thinning can unintentionally bias read_file/rg_search exposure and distort causal attribution.

risk_mitigations: Deterministic per-family retention rules with explicit floors/caps
Preserve all family classes (no full class deletion)
Retain thin contrastive anchors from dangerous legacy slices
Preserve no-call and non-targeted rows unchanged
Publish pre/post composition, tool-mix, and diversity telemetry before training

minimum_viable_retained_legacy_exposure.reasoning: Below this range in a single revision risks over-revision; above this range likely preserves confounding legacy dominance.

reasoning: Healthy conversion family share rises materially in projected balanced scenario.
Legacy ritualized shell pressure drops while remaining represented.
Procedural diversity proxies improve in projection without full legacy removal.

governance_notes: Revision must be explicitly declared as exposure-correction, not objective drift.
No hidden balancing; all selection rules must be auditable and reproducible.
No gate relaxation and no canonical eval topology changes.
